$comment: Default settings applied on plugin enable. All detector thresholds are provisional defaults from build-spec section 5.6 and are meant to be tuned by the phase 3 eval harness. Never hard-code these in detector logic; read them from here.
$statusLineNote: A plugin CANNOT auto-register a global statusline: Claude Code's plugin settings.json honors only 'agent' and 'subagentStatusLine'. Do NOT re-add a 'statusLine' key here expecting it to wire the live statusline — it is silently ignored. The live statusline is wired into the USER's own ~/.claude/settings.json by the /context-health:setup-statusline skill, which points it at a stable copy under CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA (survives plugin updates; see bin/lib/statusline-wiring.js).

context.$note: The reserve used is max(autocompactBufferTokens, windowSize * autocompactReserveFraction). 0.165 = 33000/200000, so it is exact at 200K and scales up on larger windows (a flat 33K is only ~3% of a 1M window and overstates usable space). Claude Code's real 1M autocompact point is not officially published; raise autocompactReserveFraction if your model compacts earlier. See docs/METHODOLOGY.md.

distraction.$note: contextFill bands are % of the USABLE window (after the autocompact buffer), reaching 100 at auto-compaction. yellow 50 aligns with RULER-style ~50% effective-context findings. red 85 is provisional for Claude's strong long-context models (degradation research clusters at 40-70% for smaller models, but a false alarm is worse than a miss here); calibrate against Claude-specific data in the eval harness. The '60-70% dumb zone' / 'compact at 50%' figures are practitioner heuristics, not benchmarked for Claude 200K.

confusion.$note: Error signal is precision-first: it needs at least minErrorsToFire (3) errors AND rate>threshold before firing, so routine transient failures (a failed Bash, a not-yet-created file, a flaky test) don't paint the line red. Over a 20-call window: <3 errors green; 3-4 errors (>=0.15) yellow; 5+ (>0.20) red. activeToolYellow 30 is a conservative small-model floor — frontier models tolerate far more (often 50-100+); calibrate per-model in the eval harness.

goalDrift.$calibration: Calibrated from eval/drift-pairs.json (worker/eval_drift.py): on-goal cosine floor ~0.559, drifted mean ~0.450. bge-small-en-v1.5 scores cluster in [0.6,1.0], so absolute thresholds sit low by design. yellow 0.55 is just under the on-goal floor => 0 false alarms, 93% recall; red 0.50 catches clearly-drifted pairs at the same 0 false alarms (0.45 was too low to fire usefully). The 28-pair corpus uses obvious drift; recalibrate with subtle in-domain drift pairs in the eval harness before trusting these further.
goalDrift.$weakAnchorNote: weakAnchorMinTokens is measured in whitespace-separated WORDS (~15-20 BPE tokens), not model tokens.

shadow.$note: Phase-3 CANDIDATE drift signal (research-backed 2024-2026): per-session one-sided robust modified z-score (median/MAD, finite-sample scale) of per-USER-turn cosine to the goal, gated by goal-keyword overlap, with persistence + an absolute floor for cold-start. Fixes the absolute-cutoff + pooled-blob flaws of the shipped detector. LOGGED to computed.goalDriftShadow; does NOT drive the statusline yet. OFF by default — there is no telemetry, so it only helps if YOU inspect your own sessions: enable it in your own ~/.claude/context-health-config.json to collect calibration data. Thresholds are provisional pending the Phase-3 eval harness.

contradiction.$comment: Merged clash+poisoning. Opt-in, OFF by default. Not a paid tier: when enabled it uses the user's own Claude API key or a local LLM for real contradiction/NLI detection. Zero cost until the user turns it on. Lands in Phase 3.
